prompt:
You are Agent 5 of 5 working on a codebase at /var/folders/45/myq5db_n6q5cyhtb39vkbmp80000gn/T/twining-bench-VOjd7A.

## Codebase Orientation
This is a TypeScript service. Previous sessions built the full analytics feature. Check these locations:
- `src/routes/` — API route handlers (connected in Session 4)
- `src/services/` — aggregation service implementation (Session 2)
- `src/models/` or `src/types/` — analytics data model definitions (Session 1)
- `tests/` — existing unit tests (Session 3)

Your task: Write integration tests for the full analytics pipeline and ensure everything works end-to-end.

Specifically:
1. Review the full analytics stack in `src/`: routes → handlers → aggregation service → data models.
2. Write integration tests that test the full pipeline from HTTP request to response.
3. Test key scenarios: fetching dashboard data, querying specific metrics, time-range filtering.
4. Test error scenarios: invalid parameters, missing data, unauthorized access.
5. Fix any integration issues you discover.
6. Run all tests (unit and integration) and ensure they pass.

Important:
- This is the final session — everything should work end-to-end.
- Fix integration issues but don't restructure — keep changes minimal.
- Make sure ALL tests (unit and integration) pass when you're done.

parameters.task: Write integration tests for the full analytics pipeline (routes → handlers → aggregation service → data models) and ensure all tests pass end-to-end

alternatives.option: Add HTTP framework (supertest + express) for true HTTP-level integration tests
alternatives.reason_rejected: DESIGN.md explicitly states no HTTP framework exists and adding one is out of scope. Route handlers are framework-agnostic by design.

alternatives.option: Test each layer boundary separately (repo↔service, service↔handler)
alternatives.reason_rejected: Unit tests already cover individual layers. Integration tests should exercise the full pipeline to catch cross-layer issues.

parameters.context: Agent 5 (final session) — all prior agents built the analytics stack (models, service, repository, route handlers, unit tests). Need integration tests to verify the full pipeline works end-to-end and catch any issues at layer boundaries.
parameters.rationale: Created tests/integration/analytics-pipeline.test.ts with 29 tests covering: (1) full dashboard with realistic multi-status/multi-date data, (2) empty database handling, (3) time-range filtering across the full stack, (4) period-over-period metric comparison through route handler, (5) all four metric types with consistent data, (6) time series point consistency, (7) all four granularities, (8) top products aggregation across orders, (9) custom limit and date exclusion for top products, (10) all validation error paths, (11) cross-endpoint data consistency (dashboard vs metric endpoints report same values), (12) status breakdown totals match order count, (13) realistic multi-month quarterly scenario. No integration issues found — all layers connect correctly.
parameters.summary: Integration tests for full analytics pipeline: 29 tests covering routes → service → repository → database end-to-end

parameters.detail:
Created tests/integration/analytics-pipeline.test.ts with 29 integration tests covering the full analytics pipeline (AnalyticsRouteHandler → AnalyticsService → AnalyticsRepository → Database).

Test coverage:
- Dashboard end-to-end: complete data, empty DB, time-range filtering (4 tests)
- Metric queries: period-over-period, all 4 metric types, time series, zero-previous (4 tests)
- Time-range filtering: boundary dates, all granularities, single-day, ISO datetimes (4 tests)
- Top products: multi-order aggregation, custom limit, date exclusion (3 tests)
- Error scenarios: all validation paths, error envelope consistency (10 tests)
- Cross-endpoint consistency: dashboard vs metric values, status breakdown totals, revenue consistency (3 tests)
- Realistic scenario: quarter of data with monthly granularity (1 test)

No integration issues discovered — all layers connect correctly. No code changes needed to the source files.
parameters.summary: Agent 5 complete: 29 integration tests added, all 174 tests pass (145 existing + 29 new), no integration issues found
